L1.area: Document processing and content inclusion pipeline

L2.description: A command injection vulnerability exists where a crafted include target containing special characters can bypass URI validation. The unsanitized input is subsequently passed to a Ruby I/O method that interprets pipe-prefixed strings as operating system commands, allowing arbitrary command execution on the host system when rendering user-supplied markup.

L3.area: AsciiDoc include directive processing and target URI validation
L3.description: A command injection vulnerability exists where a crafted AsciiDoc include directive target containing a pipe character and a newline can bypass the URI validation check. The unsanitized input is then passed to a Ruby I/O method that interprets pipe-prefixed strings as operating system commands, allowing arbitrary command execution on the host system when rendering user-supplied AsciiDoc markup.

ground_truth.reason: The URI validation method uses an insufficient check (Asciidoctor::Helpers.uriish?) that can be bypassed with a crafted input containing a newline, allowing pipe-prefixed commands to reach IO.foreach and URI.open which execute them as system commands.
